CheckpointRestoredData.description:
Emitted when the runtime detects and resumes from an existing LangGraph checkpoint.

``resumed_from_run_id`` identifies the previous run that created the checkpoint.
CheckpointRestoredData.title: CheckpointRestoredData

CheckpointSavedData.description:
A LangGraph checkpoint was successfully persisted.

Emitted after the agent saves checkpoint state (each completed turn when a
checkpointer is configured, or after ``command.snapshot.request``).
``label`` is an optional human-readable tag from ``command.snapshot.request``.
``run_id`` identifies the agent run that produced this checkpoint.
CheckpointSavedData.title: CheckpointSavedData

ErrorData.description:
A runtime error surfaced to the frontend. ``fatal`` kills the session; ``transient`` does
not (e.g. a tool retry, a rate-limit backoff).
ErrorData.title: ErrorData

FeedbackScoredData.description:
A user submitted feedback for a completed turn.

``run_id`` correlates to ``message.assistant.run_id``.
``rating`` is a string token (``"positive"`` / ``"negative"`` / numeric).
``comment`` and ``correct`` are optional free-text elaboration.
FeedbackScoredData.title: FeedbackScoredData

GraphNodeEnterData.description:
A LangGraph (or agloom pattern) node has started executing.

``node`` is the node name (e.g. ``"classify"``, ``"react"``, ``"synthesize"``).
``pattern`` identifies which of the 9 execution patterns owns the node.
``input_preview`` is an optional truncated preview of the node's input for
the execution-DAG pane.
GraphNodeEnterData.title: GraphNodeEnterData

GraphNodeExitData.description:
A LangGraph (or agloom pattern) node has finished.

``parent`` SHOULD be the matching ``graph.node.enter`` event id for
latency-attribution on execution-DAG visualisations.
GraphNodeExitData.title: GraphNodeExitData

HITLAllowlisted.description: User accepted *and* asked to skip future prompts for this scope (tool / pattern / worker).
HITLAllowlisted.title: HITLAllowlisted

HITLDecisionData.description:
Outcome event emitted by the runtime *after* it received and applied the response.

For ``clarification`` kind, the user's free-text answer rides in ``text``. For all other
kinds, ``decision`` is the discrete choice. Either way, ``request_id`` correlates to the
original :class:`HITLRequest`.
HITLDecisionData.title: HITLDecisionData

HITLDenied.description: User rejected; the runtime aborts the action.
HITLDenied.title: HITLDenied

HITLGranted.description: User accepted the gate; the runtime proceeds with the underlying action.
HITLGranted.title: HITLGranted

HITLRequestData.description:
Runtime asks the user to gate something. The frontend MUST reply with
``command.hitl.respond`` carrying the same ``request_id``; the runtime blocks the agent
until the response arrives (or the optional ``timeout_ms`` elapses).
HITLRequestData.title: HITLRequestData

MemoryLtRecallData.description:
Long-term memory was searched to build the query context.

``hits`` is the number of results injected. ``namespace`` is the LT namespace.
MemoryLtRecallData.title: MemoryLtRecallData

MemoryLtStoreData.description:
A fact was persisted into long-term memory.

``key`` is the storage key; ``namespace`` is the LT namespace.
MemoryLtStoreData.title: MemoryLtStoreData

MemorySessionClearedData.description: Short-term session memory for *thread* was cleared (no turns remain).
MemorySessionClearedData.title: MemorySessionClearedData

MemorySessionTurnPoppedData.description: One short-term session turn was removed (e.g. user ``/undo`` in the CLI).
MemorySessionTurnPoppedData.title: MemorySessionTurnPoppedData

MemorySessionWriteData.description:
A turn was persisted into session (short-term) memory.

``turn_count`` is the number of turns now stored for this thread.
``query_preview`` and ``output_preview`` are truncated to 200 chars.
MemorySessionWriteData.title: MemorySessionWriteData

MessageUserAttachmentSummary.description: Small attachment record for transcript replay (no raw bytes on the wire).
MessageUserAttachmentSummary.title: MessageUserAttachmentSummary

MessageUserData.description: Inbound user prompt — emitted once per ``command.invoke`` so the wire records the turn.
MessageUserData.title: MessageUserData

MetricBudgetApproachingData.description: Once per dimension when cumulative usage crosses ~80% of the configured limit.
MetricBudgetApproachingData.title: MetricBudgetApproachingData

MetricBudgetExhaustedData.description: When cumulative usage reaches the limit; runtime may block further invokes.
MetricBudgetExhaustedData.title: MetricBudgetExhaustedData

MetricCostData.description: Cost **delta** for one billable unit (LLM call, tool call, …). Currency defaults to USD.
MetricCostData.title: MetricCostData

MetricTokensData.description:
Token usage **delta** for one LLM call. Frontends sum across the session for the sidebar
rollup. ``phase`` distinguishes classifier / react / reflection / synthesizer billing.
MetricTokensData.title: MetricTokensData

OrchestrationStepData.description: Recursive pattern orchestration trace (spawn, escalate, complete).
OrchestrationStepData.title: OrchestrationStepData

PlanPreviewData.description: Classifier-only plan (``command.plan.preview``); does not run tools or patterns.
PlanPreviewData.title: PlanPreviewData

PromptCancelledData.description: The in-flight user turn will not produce a normal assistant completion.
PromptCancelledData.title: PromptCancelledData

PromptRequestedData.description: The runtime accepted a user turn and will stream agent work for this thread.
PromptRequestedData.title: PromptRequestedData

capabilities.description: Canonical capability tokens advertised by this runtime attachment.
capabilities.title: Capabilities

RuntimeFileStagedData.description: A client attachment was written under the agent working directory.
RuntimeFileStagedData.title: RuntimeFileStagedData

RuntimeMCPServersData.description: Emitted after MCP session init (may be empty if none configured).

servers.description: Per-server summary rows after connect (name, ok, error?, tool_count, tool_names, …). Empty when MCP was not attempted or the wire predates this field.
servers.title: Servers

RuntimeMCPServersData.title: RuntimeMCPServersData

session_memory_mode.description: Session rolling-memory backend from CLI/YAML before lazy agent bootstrap: ``sqlite`` | ``in-memory`` | ``none``; when ``--memory`` is omitted, ``in-memory`` (ephemeral SessionMemory matching default ``create_agent`` session memory).
session_memory_mode.title: Session Memory Mode

agent_store_kind.description: LangGraph store kind (``sqlite`` / ``memory`` / ``none`` / …); skills LT stack when not ``none``.
agent_store_kind.title: Agent Store Kind

mcp_servers_configured.description: MCP server names from ``--mcp`` / config (not yet connected until first invoke).
mcp_servers_configured.title: Mcp Servers Configured

capabilities_override.description: Optional session-level capability hints. When present, clients SHOULD apply these on top of or instead of ``runtime.config.capabilities`` per product policy.
capabilities_override.title: Capabilities Override

SessionResumedData.description:
A client has reconnected to an existing session.

Emitted instead of ``session.opened`` when the runtime detects a known
``thread_id`` (LangGraph checkpoint exists) or when a ``command.session.resume``
is received.  ``resumed_from_thread`` is the thread the client was previously
connected to.  ``replayed_from_seq`` is the first sequence number replayed from
the :class:`~agloom.protocol.store.EventStore` (absent when no replay was done).
SessionResumedData.title: SessionResumedData

SkillAppliedData.description: Skill-related context was injected into the model prompt (pre-classify catalogue).
SkillAppliedData.title: SkillAppliedData

SkillLearnedData.description: A new or updated skill was persisted to the registry.
SkillLearnedData.title: SkillLearnedData

SkillLoadedData.description: A skill's full body was loaded (typically via the ``load_skill`` tool).
SkillLoadedData.title: SkillLoadedData

TodosUpdatedData.description: Session-scoped todo list snapshot (from ``write_todos`` meta tool).
TodosUpdatedData.title: TodosUpdatedData

ToolCallResultData.description:
Tool finished successfully. ``output_preview`` is truncated; full content lives in
:class:`ToolCallStart` via correlation by ``tool_call_id``.

Optional ``diff`` carries file before/after for edit tools (wire + UI).
ToolCallResultData.title: ToolCallResultData

ToolCallStartData.description: Agent decided to invoke a tool — pre-execution. Emitted whether or not HITL gates it.
ToolCallStartData.title: ToolCallStartData

WorkerSpawnedData.description:
A worker has been created — used by SUPERVISOR / SWARM / BLACKBOARD / HYBRID_DAG patterns
to render an agent tree. ``parent_worker_id`` is set for nested supervision; root workers
leave it ``None``.
WorkerSpawnedData.title: WorkerSpawnedData

CommandAttachFileData.description:
Upload a file into the agent's CLI-tools working directory.

``content_base64`` is standard base64 (padding optional). The runtime writes to
``<working_dir>/.agloom_uploads/<id>_<sanitized_filename>`` and emits
``runtime.file.staged`` with a path relative to ``working_dir`` (POSIX slashes).
CommandAttachFileData.title: CommandAttachFileData

CommandCancelData.description:
Cancel one (or all) in-flight invocations.

When ``thread`` is supplied, only the matching invocation is cancelled.
When absent, all running invocations on this session are cancelled.
Cancellation produces ``session.closed(reason="user_aborted")`` on the wire.
CommandCancelData.title: CommandCancelData

CommandConfigSetData.description:
Hot-reload pieces of agent configuration (model, sampling, system prompt).

At least one field must be set. ``cli_tools`` is reserved for future hot-reconfiguration.
Routing pattern is chosen by the runtime classifier — not user-configurable here.

**Budget** fields (optional): set session token / USD caps (``0`` or negative clears that cap).
CommandConfigSetData.title: CommandConfigSetData

CommandFeedbackData.description:
Submit user feedback for a completed turn.

``run_id`` identifies the agent invocation to rate (from ``message.assistant.run_id``).
``rating`` is a string token: ``"positive"`` / ``"negative"`` / ``"neutral"`` or a numeric
string (``"5"``).  ``comment`` and ``correct`` carry free-text elaboration.
``metadata`` is an optional pass-through dict stored alongside the rating.
CommandFeedbackData.title: CommandFeedbackData

CommandHITLRespondData.description:
Resolve a pending ``hitl.request``.

``decision`` is one of the user-facing gate outcomes (runtime-emitted ``timeout`` /
``cancelled`` are not valid command inputs). Unknown values normalise to ``reject``.
``text`` carries a free-text answer for ``clarification`` kind gates.
CommandHITLRespondData.title: CommandHITLRespondData

CommandHarnessGitData.description: Direct harness git ops (requires runtime with harness + LT store).
CommandHarnessGitData.title: CommandHarnessGitData

CommandInvokeData.description: Start a new agent turn. ``thread`` is optional — the runtime mints one when absent.
CommandInvokeData.title: CommandInvokeData

CommandMemoryClearData.description:
Clear short-term session memory for ``thread`` (LangGraph thread id).

Omit ``thread`` only when the client cannot infer it — runtimes may reject or no-op.
CommandMemoryClearData.title: CommandMemoryClearData

CommandMemoryPopLastTurnData.description:
Pop the last short-term session-memory turn for ``thread`` (LangGraph thread id).

The runtime emits ``memory.session.turn_popped`` with the new turn count on success.
CommandMemoryPopLastTurnData.title: CommandMemoryPopLastTurnData

CommandPlanPreviewData.description: Run classifier only; emits ``plan.preview`` (no agent execution).
CommandPlanPreviewData.title: CommandPlanPreviewData

CommandProvidersListData.description: Request the curated provider catalog (slug, label, default model, env key).
CommandProvidersListData.title: CommandProvidersListData

CommandRuntimeShutdownData.description:
Graceful exit. Cancels in-flight invocations, resolves pending HITL gates
as ``cancelled``, emits ``session.closed(reason="shutdown")``, and exits.
CommandRuntimeShutdownData.title: CommandRuntimeShutdownData

CommandSchemaRequestData.description: Ask the runtime to emit ``runtime.schema`` (full AGP JSON Schema document).
CommandSchemaRequestData.title: CommandSchemaRequestData

CommandSessionCreateData.description: Mint a new opaque session id (store-backed listing uses ids once events are appended).
CommandSessionCreateData.title: CommandSessionCreateData

CommandSessionListData.description: List sessions present in the configured :class:`~agloom.protocol.store.EventStore`.
CommandSessionListData.title: CommandSessionListData

CommandSessionRenameData.description: Rewrite stored replay rows from ``from_session_id`` to ``to_session_id``.
CommandSessionRenameData.title: CommandSessionRenameData

CommandSessionResumeData.description:
Reconnect to an existing session.

The runtime emits ``session.resumed`` (not ``session.opened``) and replays
any buffered events since ``from_seq`` so the client catches up.  When
``from_seq`` is absent the runtime only emits ``session.resumed`` without
replaying past events.
CommandSessionResumeData.title: CommandSessionResumeData

CommandSnapshotRequestData.description:
Ask the runtime to save a LangGraph checkpoint and emit ``checkpoint.saved``.

``thread`` identifies which conversation thread to snapshot. When absent, the most
recently active thread on this session is used. ``label`` is a human-readable tag
stored in the checkpoint metadata.
CommandSnapshotRequestData.title: CommandSnapshotRequestData

CommandSubscribeData.description: Restrict streamed events to ``type`` prefixes (session/error/runtime/prompt/hitl always pass).
CommandSubscribeData.title: CommandSubscribeData

CommandToolInvokeData.description: Direct tool invocation (bounded — argument JSON size limits enforced by the runtime).
CommandToolInvokeData.title: CommandToolInvokeData

CommandToolListData.description: Enumerate tools exposed by the current agent (names + descriptions).
CommandToolListData.title: CommandToolListData

CommandWorkerAssignData.description:
Assign a task to a worker.

The runtime assigns work to a worker identified by ``worker_id`` and maps results
back onto the session via ``worker.*`` events. In the current single-process
runtime this is typically an in-process worker; the same command shape applies if
workers are remote peers that emit AGP back into the session.

``parent_thread`` is the supervisor's thread id — used to correlate worker
events to the originating invocation when multiple invocations run concurrently.
CommandWorkerAssignData.title: CommandWorkerAssignData

InvokeAttachment.description: Inline file payload (base64). Staged under ``.agloom/attachments/<thread>/`` by the runtime.
InvokeAttachment.title: InvokeAttachment

agp_commands.title: AGP inbound commands
agp_commands.description: Typed JSON objects sent on the NDJSON stream from client to runtime — discriminated by top-level ``type`` (``command.*``). No AGP envelope fields.

title: AGP Event
description: Agloom Protocol (AGP) v1 — discriminated union over all known event types. Parse with the 'type' field as the discriminator. Unknown 'type' values MUST be forwarded rather than rejected (forward-compat rule).
